items.label: Infra Outputs Captured — Interfaces Written
messages: Infra handoff is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:  **Created `/apps/sample-app/in-progress/infra-interfaces.md`** containing:  | Section | Contents | |---|---| | **Endpoints** | Function App, APIM Gateway, Static Web App, Cosmos DB URLs | | **Resource Names** | Resource group, Function App, APIM, SWA, Cosmos DB account, Key Vault | | **Auth Config** | Entra Tenant/Client IDs, CI/CD client IDs, CORS origins | | **Cosmos DB (Kanban)** | Account, endpoint, database (`sample-app-db`), new container (`Tasks`), partition key (`/workspaceId`), auth method | | **Terraform Outputs** | All 14 outputs with `application_insights_connection_string` masked as `<HIDDEN>` (sensitive) | | **Env Vars for Downstream** | Backend, Frontend, E2E, and Integration test environment variables |  **Security:** The `application_insights_connection_string` (marked `sensitive: true` by Terraform) was replaced with `<HIDDEN>`. All other outputs are non-sensitive resource names/URLs.

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete kanban-task-board-enhanced infra-handoff
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark infra-handoff complete"

items.label: Development Complete — Frontend
messages: Now let me explore the codebase structure and understand existing patterns.
Now let me explore the key frontend files I need to modify/reference:
Good. Now let me read the key files I'll need to reference:
Let me check the existing page patterns and E2E fixtures:
Now let me check an existing page for reference and the existing E2E test:
Let me check the CI/CD workflow for env vars compliance:

Good — the frontend deploy workflow already has `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENTRA_CLIENT_ID`, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENTRA_TENANT_ID` from infra-interfaces.md. No CI/CD changes needed for frontend.  Now I have all the context needed. Let me implement the Kanban board page, NavBar update, and E2E tests.
Now update the NavBar to add the "Task Board" link:
Now create the E2E test file:
Now let me run Jest to verify:
All tests pass. Now run the Next.js build:
Build succeeded. Now run lint:
All passing. Now verify E2E tests compile:
All 11 tests listed, including the 3 new task board tests. Now verify lockfile:
Lockfile is in sync. Now let me run the review and commit:
No breaking changes, moderate risk (50/100) which is expected for a new feature addition. Health score unchanged. Now let me commit and complete:
Now leave a doc-note and mark complete:

All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Frontend Implementation Summary  ### Files Created 1. **`frontend/src/app/tasks/page.tsx`** — Kanban task board page with:    - 3-column layout: **To Do**, **In Progress**, **Done**    - **HTML5 native drag-and-drop** (`draggable`, `dragstart`, `dragover`, `dragleave`, `drop`, `dragend`) — zero external dependencies    - **Drop zone visual feedback**: `bg-primary/10 border-dashed border-primary` on drag-over    - **Same-column drop no-op**: checks `dragSourceStatus` against target and skips API call    - **Fallback buttons**: Start (TODO→IN_PROGRESS), Done (IN_PROGRESS→DONE), Back to To Do (IN_PROGRESS→TODO), Reopen (DONE→TODO)    - **Optimistic UI**: local state updated immediately, reverted on API error    - **New task form** at top of To Do column with `Input` + `Button` from `@/components/ui/primitives`    - Accessibility: `role="list"` on columns, `role="listitem"` on cards, `aria-label` on columns    - All API calls via `apiFetch` with Zod validation using shared `@branded/schemas`  2. **`e2e/tasks.spec.ts`** — 3 Playwright E2E tests:    - Create task → drag to In Progress → reload → verify persistence    - Button fallback: Start → Done → Reopen through all statuses    - Same-column drop no-op: route intercept asserts no PATCH call  ### Files Modified - **`frontend/src/components/NavBar.tsx`** — Added "Task Board" `<Link href="/tasks">` after "About" using existing `navLinkClass` helper  ### Verification - ✅ Jest: 14/14 tests passed - ✅ Next.js build: compiled successfully, `/tasks` route generated as static page - ✅ ESLint: zero errors - ✅ E2E tests: all 11 tests (8 existing + 3 new) compile and list correctly - ✅ Lockfile: `npm ci` succeeded - ✅ No CI/CD workflow changes needed (env vars already configured)

Intent: The `@azure/cosmos` and `@azure/identity` packages aren't present despite the spec claiming they were pre-installed from the webhook-dispatcher PR. I need to add them to make the backend work.
All 24 unit tests pass. Now let me run the full test suite and type checking:
All tests pass (44 passing, 19 skipped which are the integration tests). Now type checking:
TypeScript passes clean. Now let me build and verify the esbuild output loads:
All 3 entry points load successfully. Now let me also modify the deploy-backend.yml to add the MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE setting — the spec requires this:
Now let me run the full quality gate — all tests, type checking, and lint:
